reasoning: The Lean statement asserts existence and uniqueness of a topology T' on X such that (a) T' contains every T i (as a set of open sets, via ⊆), and (b) T' is contained in every other topology T'' that contains all T i. This is exactly the standard encoding of "smallest topology containing all T_α". The use of ∃! is appropriate because the property "smallest element of a poset" uniquely determines T' whenever it holds (by antisymmetry of ⊆), so existence-with-uniqueness matches the informal "unique smallest". Types match: topologies as Set (Set X), containment as ⊆, family indexed by I. No hypotheses are added or dropped relative to the informal claim.
